protocol.name: wlr_virtual_pointer_unstable_v1
protocol.copyright:
Copyright 2017 The wlr-protocols contributors Copyright 2017 Drew DeVault
Permission to use, copy, modify, and/or distribute this software for any purpose with or without fee is hereby granted, provided that the above copyright notice and this permission notice appear in all copies.

interface.name: zwlr_virtual_pointer_manager_v1
interface.description:
This protocol allows clients to emulate a physical pointer device.
The requests are mostly mirror opposites of those specified in wl_pointer. A client would request a zwlr_virtual_pointer_v1 object from this interface.
The compositor will then emulate a pointer device, and it will emit events to the clients, as if a physical device was used.

request.name: create_virtual_pointer
request.description: Create a new virtual pointer object.

request.name: create_virtual_pointer_with_output

description.summary: create a new virtual pointer with a given output

request.description:
Create a new virtual pointer object with a given output.
The compositor will then emulate a pointer device, and it will emit events to the clients, as if a physical device was used. If the output argument is NULL, a default output is chosen by the compositor. Otherwise, if the output is not NULL, the compositor should map the input device to the requested output.

request.name: destroy

description.summary: destroy the virtual pointer manager object

request.description: Destroy the virtual pointer manager object.

interface.name: zwlr_virtual_pointer_v1
interface.description: This object allows clients to create individual virtual pointer objects.

request.name: motion
request.description: The pointer has moved by a relative amount to the previous request. Values are in the global compositor space.

request.name: motion_absolute
request.description: The pointer has moved in an absolute coordinate frame. Value of x can range from 0 to x_extent, value of y can range from 0 to y_extent. Where x_extent and y_extent are the width and height of the output assigned to the virtual pointer.

request.name: button
request.description: A button was pressed or released.

arg.name: value
arg.summary: length of vector in fixed point value

request.name: frame

description.summary: end of a pointer event sequence

request.description: Indicates the set of events that logically belong together.

request.name: axis_source

description.summary: source information for scroll and other axis

request.description: Source information for scroll and other axis.

request.name: axis_stop

description.summary: stop notification for scroll and other axes

request.description: Stop notification for scroll and other axes.

request.name: axis_discrete

description.summary: discrete step information for scroll and other axes

request.description:
Discrete step information for scroll and other axes.
This event allows the client to extend data normally sent using the axis event with discrete value.
